en: Respirator-related medical examinations must address frequent or prolonged use of respirators. Medical evaluation must consider physiological burden.\nEmployee Protection Act, Section 49(2)
Respiratory protection must be provided when hazardous substances or oxygen deficiency risks exist.\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 15(2)
Respiratory protection must reduce inhalation exposure below occupational limit values.\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 15(3)
Respiratory PPE instruction must include use, maintenance, and functional checks. Training must include proper donning and doffing.\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 15(6), 15(7)(1)

Drills on donning and doffing respiratory protective equipment must be conducted at intervals of no more than six months. During these drills, instruction on functional checks must be repeated.\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 15(7)(2)
Respiratory devices must be inspected at least quarterly.\nPersonal Protective Equipment Ordinance, Section 15(8)(1)
